phase1: Identify all Server Components and Client Components in the affected file tree
Map 'use client' boundaries — count directives and assess whether they are minimal
Trace data-fetching patterns: async Server Components, fetch() caching, generateStaticParams
Document Server Actions (<form action>, 'use server' functions) and their validation approach
Check for hydration risk: browser-only APIs (window, localStorage) in shared components
Identify Layout vs Page component hierarchy and any parallel/intercepting routes
phase3: Minimize 'use client' — push state as low as possible; prefer Server Components for data display
Use Server Actions for mutations; validate input server-side with zod or similar
Co-locate data fetching in Server Components; avoid prop-drilling fetched data through Client Components
Handle loading.tsx and error.tsx at route segments; never block the entire layout
Cache fetch() calls at the request level; use revalidatePath/revalidateTag for on-demand ISR
Avoid 'use client' in layout.tsx unless absolutely required for global state providers
phase4: Test Server Components with React Testing Library in a Node environment (no browser globals)
Test Server Actions independently as plain async functions with mocked DB/service layers
Test Client Components with userEvent and mocked server action stubs
Use Playwright for end-to-end route rendering and navigation tests
Assert hydration errors are absent in Playwright console logs
